prior.content_md:
**MONITORING — EASA Digital Reporting Tool and Delegated Acts.** EASA has been designated as the authority hosting the digital reporting infrastructure for the regulation. The EASA ReFuelEU Aviation Digital Reporting Tool page indicates this infrastructure is in development for compliance year reporting. Delegated acts from the European Commission are expected to specify detailed reporting formats, SAF sustainability verification methodologies, and the exact accounting rules for synthetic fuel sub-mandates.

- **Why it matters to the workspace:** Delegated acts specifying how SAF volumes are attributed to individual flights could affect the granularity of SAF surcharge documentation that airlines provide to cargo customers. If attribution methodology is flight-specific, the workspace may be able to obtain per-shipment SAF documentation for client sustainability reporting.
- **Expected issuing body:** European Commission (delegated act) and EASA (reporting tool).
- **Status:** No confirmed publication date identified in available sources as of the date of this brief. Legal Confirmation Required on whether any delegated acts have been formally adopted and published subsequent to the regulation's entry into force date.

*Source: EASA ReFuelEU Aviation Digital Reporting Tool & Regulation Repository, European Union Aviation Safety Agency. https://www.easa.europa.eu/en/domains/environment/refueleu-aviation-digital-reporting-tool*
*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Articles 11–12 (delegated act provisions), Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

---

**MONITORING — Fit for 55 EU transport emissions harmonisation framework.** A political agreement was reached in November 2025 on new EU rules to harmonise transport emissions calculations. This framework, if adopted as regulation, would establish how emissions from transport services — including air freight — are measured and reported across the EU. If adopted, it would affect how the workspace documents and reports Scope 3 emissions from air freight to clients.

- **Why it matters:** The workspace's clients in luxury goods, fine art, and live events are increasingly subject to emissions disclosure requirements. A harmonised EU methodology for air freight emissions accounting would determine the standard against which SAF-related emissions reductions are credited, affecting the commercial value of SAF surcharge expenditure as a client sustainability credential.
- **Expected issuing body:** European Parliament and Council (formal adoption pending).
- **Status:** Political agreement reached as of November 2025 per Commission announcement; formal adoption and publication pending as of the date of this brief.

*Source: Commission welcomes political agreement on new rules to harmonise transport emissions calculations in the EU, European Commission DG MOVE, 6 November 2025. https://transport.ec.europa.eu/news-events/news/commission-welcomes-political-agreement-new-rules-harmonise-transport-emissions-calculations-eu-2025-11-06_en*

---
